# Address Quality Pipeline for Large-Scale Patient Geocoding

A reproducible pipeline for identifying, classifying, and quantifying text biases in geocoded patient addresses at scale. Developed in the context of a French hospital system using the [Base Adresse Nationale (BAN)](https://adresse.data.gouv.fr/) geocoder.

---

## Context

When patient addresses are geocoded at scale, the geocoder silently drops information it cannot resolve: apartment numbers, building identifiers, institutional names, care facility labels. This creates a systematic gap between the raw address recorded in the hospital information system and the address that was actually used to produce a GPS coordinate.

This pipeline makes that gap measurable. It identifies which types of extra information are most common, locates addresses that are systematically misreferenced (hotspots), and quantifies the socio-economic error introduced when the geocoded location falls in a different IRIS zone than the true one.

---

## What the pipeline does

The pipeline runs in four sequential steps.

**Step 1 — Text bias identification** reads raw patient addresses and their BAN geocoding results, aligns them using a Needleman-Wunsch sequence algorithm, and separates orthographic errors (typos resolved by geocoding) from genuine extra information not handled by the geocoder. The most frequent extra tokens across the dataset are identified as the operational bias list.

**Step 2 — Hotspot identification** detects addresses that appear an abnormally high number of times in the dataset — typically institutional or administrative addresses (care homes, hospitals) where many patients share the same official address. These are flagged for manual review before being excluded from bias analysis.

**Step 3 — Reference bias integration** takes the bias list from step 1 and injects each bias into a public reference address dataset (French school addresses). Each biased variant is geocoded via the BAN API. The difference between the reference coordinate and the geocoded coordinate measures the spatial impact of each bias.

**Step 4 — Results analysis** joins geocoded points to IRIS zones, attaches median disposable income from the INSEE Filosofi dataset, and computes the income difference between the true IRIS zone and the geocoded IRIS zone. This quantifies the socio-economic error introduced by each type of text bias.

---

## Repository structure

```
address-quality-pipeline/
├── README.md
├── LICENSE.md               project licence + third-party attributions (addok/BAN/IGN/INSEE)
├── environment.yml          conda environment (RAPIDS 24.12, Python 3.12)
├── requirements.txt
├── sample.csv               anonymized example input (reference addresses)
│
├── notebooks/               exploratory reference notebooks (01–04)
├── scripts/                 terminal-ready pipeline scripts
│   ├── run_pipeline.py      master entry point
│   ├── build_iris_geojson.py   one-time IRIS shapefile → GeoJSON conversion
│   ├── 01_textbiais_identification.py
│   ├── 02_hotspot_identification.py
│   ├── 03_ref_biaised_integration.py
│   └── 04_results_analysis.py
│
├── src/                     shared library (GPU/CPU unified)
│   ├── methods.py
│   └── chunkcsv.py
│
└── data/
    ├── odonymes.txt          lane-type synonym table (reference, included)
    ├── Prenoms.csv           French given-name list (reference, included)
    ├── biaises_identified.csv   generated by step 01
    ├── identified_admin_bias.csv   generated by step 02, reviewed manually
    ├── socioeco/             NOT included — place your files here
    │   ├── iris.geojson         generate with build_iris_geojson.py
    │   └── BASE_TD_FILO_DISP_IRIS_2020.csv   INSEE Filosofi
    ├── reference/            NOT included — place your files here
    │   └── fr-en-adresse-et-geolocalisation-etablissements-*.csv
    └── outputs/              generated outputs (gitignored)
```

Patient CSV data is never committed to this repository (CNIL compliance). Only anonymized reference files and pipeline code are included.

---

## Requirements

The pipeline has two hardware profiles.

Steps 01 and 03 use GPU acceleration via [RAPIDS cuDF](https://rapids.ai/) for large-scale string operations. A CUDA-capable GPU is required for these steps. Steps 02 and 04 run on CPU only.

```bash
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate rapids-24.12
```

Key dependencies: `cudf`, `cuspatial`, `geopandas`, `rapidfuzz`, `phonetic-fr`, `python-Levenshtein`, `libpostal` (optional, improves normalization).

---

## Data requirements

| File | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Geocoded patient CSV | Hospital information system | Not included (patient data) |
| `odonymes.txt` | Included | Lane-type synonym table |
| `Prenoms.csv` | Included | French given-name reference |
| `CONTOURS-IRIS.shp` | [IGN](https://geoservices.ign.fr/irisge) | Convert once with `build_iris_geojson.py` |
| `BASE_TD_FILO_DISP_IRIS_2020.csv` | [INSEE Filosofi](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/6036907) | Median income by IRIS zone |
| Reference address CSV | [data.gouv.fr](https://data.gouv.fr) — school addresses | Public dataset |

---

## Quick start

```bash
# 1. Clone / set up the repository
git clone <repository-url>
cd address-quality-pipeline

# 2. Create and activate the conda environment - RAPIDS requires a specific CUDA version. 
# Check yours first:
nvidia-smi | head -4
# Look for: CUDA Version: XX.X

conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate adr_env
## conda install conda-forge::libpostal -- install lippostal separatly 

# 3. Configure GPU selection
# The scripts set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES before importing cudf, so no manual
# configuration is needed. The default is GPU 2 (matching the original notebook).
# To use a different GPU, pass --gpu at the command line:
python scripts/01_textbiais_identification.py data/sample.csv --gpu 0

# To check available GPUs:
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,name,memory.total --format=csv

# 4. Geocoding setup
# Define your serveur URL : 
export ADDOK_URL="http://egp-srvbigdata1.egp.aphp.fr:7878/search/csv/" # or localhost etc. depending on where you installed it


# 2. Run the full pipeline
python scripts/run_pipeline.py path/to/patients_geocoded.csv --gpu 2

# 3. After step 02 completes, review the hotspot candidate file before continuing
#    Open data/identified_admin_bias.csv, remove false positives, save.

# 4. Run steps 03 and 04
python scripts/run_pipeline.py path/to/patients_geocoded.csv --steps 3,4
```

The pipeline code and reference data files (`odonymes.txt`, `Prenoms.csv`,
`sample.csv`) are the authors' own work. See `LICENSE.md` to apply a licence —
MIT and Etalab Open Licence 2.0 are both suggested options.

| [addok](https://github.com/addok/addok) | Geocoding engine behind the BAN API | MIT |
| [API Adresse / BAN](https://adresse.data.gouv.fr) | National address reference dataset | Etalab Open Licence 2.0 |
| [INSEE Filosofi](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/6036907) | IRIS median income data | Etalab Open Licence 2.0 |
| [IGN CONTOURS-IRIS](https://geoservices.ign.fr/irisge) | IRIS boundary polygons | Etalab Open Licence 2.0 |

Patient data is never included in this repository (CNIL compliance).
